# BUG-007 Writeup
> Generated by [Quality Playbook](https://github.com/andrewstellman/quality-playbook) v1.4.6 — Andrew Stellman
> Date: 2026-04-20 · Project: virtio-1.4.6

## 1. Summary

REQ-011 requires setup-time affinity programming to follow the queue index that vDPA queue discovery actually instantiated, but `drivers/virtio/virtio_vdpa.c:350-363` creates queues with `queue_idx++` and then applies affinity using the sparse descriptor slot `i`.

## 2. Spec reference

`include/linux/virtio_config.h:22-33` documents that `virtqueue_info.name == NULL` means an unused queue slot, so sparse queue arrays are a supported input shape rather than an invalid caller state.

## 3. The code

`virtio_vdpa_find_vqs()` skips unnamed slots, compacts queue creation via `virtio_vdpa_setup_vq(vdev, queue_idx++, ...)`, and then immediately calls `ops->set_vq_affinity(vdpa, i, &masks[i])`. The same file's steady-state affinity helper uses `vq->index` as the authoritative queue identity (`drivers/virtio/virtio_vdpa.c:407-418`), proving the setup path is using the wrong namespace.

## 4. Observable consequence

When a driver leaves holes in `virtqueue_info[]` and also provides an affinity descriptor, the backend receives a CPU mask for the sparse caller slot instead of the compact queue that was instantiated. That silently pins the wrong queue to the wrong CPU mask and can misroute completion affinity without any queue-creation failure.

## 5. The fix

Use the just-created queue's actual index when programming affinity:

```diff
diff --git a/drivers/virtio/virtio_vdpa.c b/drivers/virtio/virtio_vdpa.c
index 7777777..8888888 100644
--- a/drivers/virtio/virtio_vdpa.c
+++ b/drivers/virtio/virtio_vdpa.c
@@ -359,7 +359,7 @@ static int virtio_vdpa_find_vqs(struct virtio_device *vdev, unsigned int nvqs,
 			goto err_setup_vq;
 		}
 
-		if (has_affinity)
-			ops->set_vq_affinity(vdpa, i, &masks[i]);
+		if (has_affinity)
+			ops->set_vq_affinity(vdpa, vqs[i]->index, &masks[i]);
 	}
```

## 6. The test

- **Requirement:** REQ-011
- **Regression test:** `quality/test_regression.py::TestCodeReviewRegressions.test_bug_007_vdpa_affinity_uses_instantiated_queue_index`
- **Regression patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-007-regression-test.patch`
- **Fix patch:** `quality/patches/BUG-007-fix.patch`
- **Expected behavior:** the regression fails on the current tree because setup-time affinity still uses `i`, then passes once the fix patch switches to `vqs[i]->index`.

## 7. FAIL -> PASS evidence

```text
RED: quality/results/BUG-007.red.log
GREEN: quality/results/BUG-007.green.log
```
